title: melicope nealae
intro: Melicope nealae, also known as the Hawaiian mountain garlic, is a flowering plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Melicope nealae include its ability to aid in the treatment of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, including tea, tincture, and poultice, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
